Name: spectral_inference_optimization
Title: Spectral Inference Optimization: A Training-Free Framework for Latency-Critical Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that spectral methods can be used to optimize machine learning inference in latency-critical applications, by identifying and leveraging the most informative spectral directions in the data.
Related Work: Our work builds upon recent advances in training-free machine learning methods, such as Spectral Latent Steering, Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, and Spectral Quantization Routing. We differentiate our proposal by focusing on the use of spectral methods for inference optimization, rather than just precision allocation or interference cancellation.
Abstract: We propose a training-free framework for latency-critical machine learning inference, based on spectral methods. Our approach identifies the most informative spectral directions in the data and leverages them to optimize inference. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmarks, showing improved accuracy and reduced latency compared to existing methods. Our framework is grounded in theoretical bounds, ensuring transparency and reproducibility.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on several latency-critical benchmarks, including image classification and natural language processing tasks.
Compare the performance of our approach with existing methods, including Spectral Latent Steering and Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation.
Investigate the effect of different spectral methods, such as PCA and randomized SVD, on the performance of our framework.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may not be effective for all types of data or applications, and may require careful tuning of hyperparameters.
The use of spectral methods may introduce additional computational overhead, which could impact the overall latency of the system.
The framework may not be robust to adversarial attacks or distributional shifts, which could impact its performance in real-world applications.
